name: GPT-5.6 Sol
codingSpecialization: Complex coding, computer use, research, cybersecurity
bestUse: Architecture, hardest debugging, major refactors, high-value autonomous work
avoidFor: Routine edits when quota efficiency matters

name: GPT-5.6 Terra
codingSpecialization: Everyday coding, tools, balanced intelligence and cost
bestUse: Default feature work, review, debugging, medium refactors
avoidFor: Only the most ambiguous frontier tasks

name: GPT-5.6 Luna
codingSpecialization: Fast, high-volume, well-scoped coding and transformations
bestUse: Focused edits, code search, repetitive implementation, high-volume subagents
avoidFor: Very large repositories requiring reliable deep-context retrieval

name: GLM-5.2
codingSpecialization: Long-horizon coding, terminal work, large repositories
bestUse: Open-model architecture, difficult terminal work, million-token repositories
avoidFor: High-volume routine edits where cheaper Go models dominate

name: GLM-5.1
avoidFor: New work: GLM-5.2 has same Go pricing and much higher capability

name: Kimi K2.7 Code
toolSupport: Strong orchestration; no Go-native swarm claim
bestUse: Long difficult implementations, multi-step coding, frontend and performance work

name: Kimi K2.6
toolSupport: Native Agent Swarm up to 300 subagents
codingSpecialization: Coding, frontend, agent swarm and long-running execution
bestUse: Agent orchestration, UI work, long-running full-stack execution
avoidFor: Maximum value when K2.7 Code is available for pure coding

name: MiMo-V2.5
codingSpecialization: Low-cost agent work, exploration and everyday coding
bestUse: Repository exploration, small edits, bulk execution, multimodal tasks
avoidFor: Hardest architecture and long autonomous debugging

name: MiMo-V2.5-Pro
bestUse: Cost-conscious complex coding, review, multimodal agent workflows
avoidFor: Bulk work where standard MiMo gives roughly nine times more requests

name: MiniMax M3
bestUse: Complete app builds, frontend from media, long autonomous implementation
avoidFor: Terminal-heavy tasks where Qwen Plus or GLM is stronger

name: MiniMax M2.7
codingSpecialization: Cost-effective project construction and autonomous work
bestUse: Economical project construction and autonomous iteration
avoidFor: Terminal reliability and multimodal work; M3 supersedes it

name: Qwen3.7 Max
codingSpecialization: Architecture, reasoning, coding and long-horizon tools
bestUse: Architecture, difficult algorithms, long-context tool use and review
avoidFor: Routine development because Qwen3.7 Plus offers far higher usage

name: Qwen3.7 Plus
codingSpecialization: Terminal-heavy development, review and everyday coding
bestUse: Best Go default: debugging, terminal work, review and general development
avoidFor: Only the hardest architecture where Max or GLM-5.2 is justified

name: Qwen3.6 Plus
codingSpecialization: Older multimodal coding and agent workflows
avoidFor: New workflows: Qwen3.7 Plus is stronger and cheaper

name: DeepSeek V4 Pro
codingSpecialization: Algorithms, difficult logical bugs and terminal reasoning
bestUse: Algorithms, system debugging, backend logic and terminal tasks
avoidFor: Visual UI implementation and tasks requiring confirmed multimodal support

name: DeepSeek V4 Flash
codingSpecialization: Ultra-low-cost code generation, exploration and bulk work
bestUse: Search, boilerplate, tests, documentation and precisely scoped bulk edits
avoidFor: High-stakes refactors and long ambiguous debugging

name: Grok 4.5
codingSpecialization: Flagship coding, terminal work, agentic tools, frontend and general engineering
bestUse: Fast frontier coding, Terminal-Bench-style work, tools, debugging and multimodal implementation
avoidFor: Work requiring a one-million-token context window or the most mature enterprise controls
